free_text: Levels in 23Na excited by the 19F(alpha,alpha)19F,
19F(alpha,gamma)23Na and 19F(alpha,p)22Ne reactions
preliminary publication.
English transl. of IZV.
SD
VS. References IZV and BAS added.
Unit in 003 corrected.
VT Major revision in 005-007
Thin targets were fabricated by evaporating strontium
fluoride (SrF2) on thin self-supporting carbon foils.
The experimental resolution was 6 keV in the
c.m. system at Egamma = 2.35 MeV.
Six surface-barrier detectors. Five of them
were situated at lab. ang. 78., 114.5, 132., 142. and
170. with respect to the direction of the incoming
beam. The sixth detector at 160. was used for
dead-time and yield monitoring purposes.

The (alpha,po) curve was obtained from the
charged-particle spectra of the detector at 170 degr.
For the other angles the yield of the (alpha,po)
reaction was too small to extract additional
information.

Thin targets were fabricated by evaporating strontium
fluoride (SrF2) on thin self-supporting carbon foils.
The gamma-ray excitation functions were
measured by 7.5 cm diam. x 7.5 cm NaI crystal
at 90. degr.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
The data on figure are given in Counts per 500 micro-
Coulomb. The compiler multiplied data by 500.
Curves from fig. 2 of the article
Thick targets of 40 keV thickness at Ealpha = 2.1 MeV
evaporated onto 0.3 mm thick Cu backings were used
for the capture reaction.
The gamma-ray excitation functions were
remeasured below Ealpha= 2.3 MeV by
12.5 cm diam. X 12.5 cm NaI crystal on a thick target
positioned at the center of a turntable.
A 105 cm3 Ge(Li) detector (of 2.7 keV resolution at
1.33 MeV) measure the gamma-ray spectra.
In the gamma-ray angular distribution measurement
(at five angles) 10 cm diam. X 10 cm NaI and 25 cm3
Ge(Li) detectors were used moving and monitoring
detectors, respectively.
The data on figure are given in Counts per 500 micro-
Coulomb. The compiler multiplied data by 500.
Curves from fig. 2 of the article
